issues.detail: The instructions do not cover error handling for email sending or missing environment variables, limiting robustness.
The code examples are specific to Node.js/TypeScript, limiting applicability to other languages or AI agents.

scenarios.name: GDPR-compliant weekly report send with tracking and tags
scenarios.rationale: Treatment uses mailgun.js with the EU endpoint (api.eu.mailgun.net), passes o:tag, o:tracking-clicks, and o:tracking-opens options on messages.create, and validates recipients with mg.validate.get before send; baseline sends a basic email without EU routing or Mailgun tracking options.

scenarios.name: Webhook handler that verifies HMAC and reacts to bounces/complaints
scenarios.rationale: Treatment verifies the signature with HMAC-SHA256 over timestamp+token using MAILGUN_WEBHOOK_KEY, then switches on event-data event types including 'failed' with severity='permanent' and 'complained' to suppress/unsubscribe; baseline accepts the webhook without HMAC verification and misses the permanent-bounce and complaint branches.
